title: Stanfieldia nealleyi
taxonomy: Stanfieldia nealleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States. The classification of Stanfieldia nealleyi is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and then into its specific taxonomic classification: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Ericales, family Ericaceae, genus Stanfieldia, species S. nealleyi. The classification is an important aspect of understanding the evolutionary history and relationships of the plant. This classification is crucial for identifying and differentiating the species from other closely related species.

intro: Stanfieldia nealleyi, also known as the Nealley's tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pentaphyllaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stanfieldia nealleyi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Stanfieldia nealleyi is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
